metadata.description: GraphQL: Client declares fields. Single endpoint. Avoid over-fetching or under-fetching. Here is a brief Python code example to illustrate the concept of using a single endpoint in API calls, which can help avoid over-fetching or under-fetching data: ```python import requests def fetch_data(url): # Make a GET request to the single endpoint response = requests.get(url) # Check if the request was successful if response.status_code == 200: # Assuming the API returns JSON, parse it data = response.json() # Client can declare fields needed and extract them here required_fields = ['id', 'name', 'price'] # Example fields to fetch fetched_data = {field: data.get(field) for field in required_fields} return fetched_data else: print(f"Failed to retrieve data: {response.status_code}") return None # Example usage url = "https://api.example.com/single-endpoint" data = fetch_data(url) print(data) ``` This example demonstrates making a single API call and fetching only the necessary fields, which aligns with the concept of avoiding over-fetching or under-fetching data.
